---
title: "Benjamin R. Baer"
image: "baer-web.jpeg"
links:
  - label: Google Scholar
    url: "https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=ECPfj-QAAAAJ"
  - label: LinkedIn
    url: "https://www.linkedin.com/in/benjamin-baer/"
  - label: Email
    url: "mailto:b.baer@bowdoin.edu"
output:
  postcards::trestles:
    includes:
      in_header: google-verify.html
---

## Bio

Hi, my name is Ben. I am an Assistant Professor in the
Department of Mathematics at [Bowdoin College](https://www.bowdoin.edu/math/index.html/) in Maine and an Honorary Lecturer in Statistics at the [University of St Andrews](https://stats.wp.st-andrews.ac.uk)
in Scotland.  

My research interests are broadly in applied/methodological statistics, with a particular focus on incomplete data problems such as causal inference, survival analysis, and ecological survey methods, as well as efficient estimation.
Much of my work involves developing statistical methods tailored to the scientific questions that motivate them.

## Education

**Cornell University** | Ithaca, NY  
Ph.D. and M.S. in Statistics | September 2015 – August 2021  
Supervised by [Marty Wells](https://stat.cornell.edu/people/faculty/martin-wells)

**Millersville University** | Millersville, PA  
B.S. in Mathematics | September 2011 – May 2015  
Advised by [James Fenwick](https://www.millersville.edu/math/faculty/) and [Zhigang Han](https://www.millersville.edu/math/faculty/)

## Experience

**Bowdoin College** | Assistant Professor | July 2026 – Present

**University of St Andrews** | Lecturer (Assistant Professor) | August 2023 – June 2026

**University of Rochester** | Postdoctoral Fellow | August 2021 – July 2023

**Weill Cornell Medicine** | Research Data Scientist | April 2020 – August 2021

**Amazon** | Applied Scientist | May 2019 – August 2019
